title: 401(k)
content: A 401(k) is an employer-sponsored retirement savings account that may include employer matching contributions. Contributions may be pre-tax or Roth depending on plan options.

title: Traditional IRA
content: A Traditional IRA is an individual retirement account that may offer tax-deductible contributions depending on income and other retirement coverage rules.

title: Roth IRA
content: A Roth IRA is a retirement account funded with after-tax dollars, allowing qualified withdrawals in retirement to be tax free under current rules.

title: Retirement Savings Goal
content: A retirement savings plan should consider current age, target retirement age, expected annual expenses, inflation, and estimated investment returns.

title: Emergency Fund Before Investing
content: Many financial plans recommend building an emergency fund covering 3 to 6 months of essential expenses before taking on higher-risk investing goals.
